source_pr.title: test: fix unstable TestPessimisticLockOnPartition
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Fixes https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/18933 https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/18222 https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/18039 https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/17146 https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/16435 https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/15182

The last `commit` statement may be executed after the table is dropped.

### What is changed and how it works?

Wait for the goroutine to quit before quit the test function.

### Related changes

- Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- no release note

symptoms: `TestPessimisticLockOnPartition` is unstable because a background goroutine may still be committing after the main test drops the table.
failure_signature: Async goroutine commit races with test teardown (DROP TABLE), leading to intermittent failures
root_cause_explanation: The test spawns a goroutine that performs DML and `COMMIT` and uses a channel to coordinate the expected blocking/unblocking order. Previously, the main test only waited for part of the goroutine progress and could proceed to teardown (including dropping the table) while the goroutine was still running, so the final `COMMIT` could execute after the table was dropped. This PR stabilizes the test by adding an extra channel signal after `COMMIT` and waiting for it, ensuring the goroutine fully exits before teardown.
fix_pattern: Wait for background goroutines to fully finish (via channel/WaitGroup sync) before dropping tables or ending the test to avoid teardown races.

evidence: evidence: PR title: fix unstable TestPessimisticLockOnPartition.
evidence: updates session tests to reduce nondeterminism in pessimistic lock on partition scenario (session/session_test.go).
